77.reason: [The loop condition uses '\0' as a sentinel, but a[] is an array of long long int, not a string. This will cause undefined behavior or incorrect termination, potentially missing or misreading array elements.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop condition uses '\0' as a sentinel, but a[] is an array of long long int, not a string. This will cause undefined behavior or incorrect termination, potentially missing or misreading array elements.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition `a[i]!='\0'` is incorrect because `a` is an array of `long long int`, not a string. This will not properly terminate the loop, potentially reading uninitialized values or causing undefined behavior. The loop should iterate up to `p` (the number of elements stored).
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition stops at the first zero element, but a[0] becomes 0 due to multiplication by zero, so the loop never executes and sum remains 0.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition a[i]!='\0' treats any zero value as end-of-array sentinel, causing premature termination when earlier terms are zero (as in test case). Should iterate up to p (number of terms) instead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition `a[i]!='\0'` is incorrect because `a` is an array of `long long int`, not characters. It should iterate up to `p` (the number of terms). Using `'\0'` (which is 0) as a terminator is unsafe because valid calculation results can be 0, causing the loop to terminate prematurely or access uninitialized memory if no zero is encountered.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition `a[i]!='\0'` is incorrect because `a` is an array of `long long int`, not a null-terminated string. It should iterate up to `p` (inclusive) to sum all calculated terms. Using `'\0'` (which is 0) as a terminator is unsafe and logically wrong here, especially if any term evaluates to 0 or if memory contains non-zero values beyond `p`.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The array `a` is not null-terminated, so this loop condition is invalid and leads to undefined behavior by reading uninitialized memory beyond the actual number of terms.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The array `a` is not null-terminated, so this loop may read uninitialized memory beyond the actual number of terms, leading to undefined behavior or wrong results. The loop should run only up to `p+1` terms instead of checking for '\0'.]

76.reason: [This line applies the sign `z` to the last term, but `a[p]` may not have been initialized to 0 for new terms, leading to garbage values being used in accumulation. Also, the logic fails when the expression starts with multiplication/division because `a[p]` is not properly initialized before use.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This line applies the sign `z` only to the last term, but terms processed by * or / already had their signs applied incorrectly earlier. The logic of handling signs and multiplicative operations is flawed because `z` is only applied when encountering '+' or '-', but multiplicative terms are stored in `a[p]` without considering the current sign context.]
